sessions.title: Set up testing and debugging in NETApp with VSCode
sessions.outcome: Set up VS Code testing/debugging for NETapp, added a unit test with Postman mocking guidance, and fixed a Git PATH issue blocking the app — landed successfully.

sessions.outcome: Added per-run logging, time-of-day picker for day-based cron schedules, and out-of-band change protection to the scheduler app; verified live and landed successfully.

sessions.title: Set up testing and debugging in VSCode for NETApp
sessions.outcome: Set up xUnit testing and VS Code debugging for NETapp, added a Postman-mocking test for OriginationService, and diagnosed missing PATH entries (dotnet, git) causing tool failures; landed successfully.

resume
will you build it?
commands: cd C:/Sandbox && git add .gitignore package.json apps/markitdown/service/main.py apps/markitdown/service/ui.html apps/ma
cd C:/Sandbox && git push origin main
sessions.outcome: That's the launcher checking for the venv at startup. Two paths depending on what's on that server: **Python is available there:** ``` npm run markitdown:setup ``` That's it — creates the venv and installs markitdown. One-time, then it auto-starts. **No Python on that server:** Set env vars to point the Node app at the Python service running somewhere else: ``` MARKITDOWN_LAUNCH=external MARKITDOW

sessions.title: Write prompt for port catalog CLI routing
prompts: look at port catalog -> route through cli seam write the prompt. first variant is for node server. claude-engine does not exist on the target server. avoid specific references or links that appear on
is port available on github
yes
add an entry for markitdown core app. Assume UI is to be kept as is.
sessions.outcome: Done. Entry is live at [jdevins/sandbox](https://github.com/jdevins/sandbox). The prompt is structured in four steps: 1. **policy.json** — the JSON verbatim, since it's the source of truth for both layers 2. **Python service** — `main.py` contract (endpoints, tempfile pattern, .csv fallback, shutdown hook, standalone entry point) + `requirements.txt` 3. **Node lib files** — `env.js`, `launcher.js`

sessions.outcome: Done. `hello` and `guestbook` are gone, committed on `main`.

summary:
Tools - Designed and authored a portable export of the MarkItDown tool so a capability built in one environment can run standalone elsewhere

Governance - Stood up an evolving standards catalog with automated enforcement at commit time and a Claude-authored proposal path for new rules

Automation - Extended the session-reporting pipeline to filter, batch, and summarize work history on demand instead of one entry at a time

Focused on packaging a working prototype into a portable, shareable tool and building the governance scaffolding for Claude-assisted work to scale safely and consistently

Count of session: 36
Count of automations ran: 0
